sourceTitle: How managers get a clear view of what everyone’s working on in Notion

questions.question: What is the main purpose of creating a company‑wide wiki in Notion?

options.text: To store all financial transactions of the company
options.explanation: Financial data belongs in accounting software, not a wiki.

options.text: To provide a centralized place for shared information like policies, mission, and company goals
options.explanation: The wiki is designed for easy access to core company information, reducing the need to ask managers for basics.

options.text: To replace all project‑management tools used by individual teams
options.explanation: A wiki complements project‑management databases; it does not replace them.

options.text: To automatically generate performance reviews for each employee
options.explanation: Performance reviews require dedicated data and processes, not just a static wiki.

questions.question: Which Notion property type should you use to indicate who is responsible for a project?

options.text: Date
options.explanation: Date tracks deadlines, not people.

options.text: Select
options.explanation: Select lets you choose from predefined labels, but not tag a specific workspace member.

options.text: Person
options.explanation: The Person property lets you @‑mention a teammate, making them the project owner.

options.text: Number
options.explanation: Number is for numeric values, not for assigning people.

questions.question: Which database view is most useful for quickly spotting upcoming project deadlines?

options.text: Board view grouped by Status
options.explanation: Board view shows workflow stages, not calendar dates.

options.text: Calendar view
options.explanation: Calendar view places each project on its deadline date, making upcoming due dates obvious.

options.text: Table view sorted by Project Owner
options.explanation: Sorting by owner helps workload balance, not deadline visibility.

options.text: Timeline view
options.explanation: Timeline shows duration but requires start/end dates; calendar is simpler for single deadlines.

questions.question: How does grouping a board view by "Project owner" help a manager monitor workload?

options.text: It automatically reassigns tasks to balance the load.
options.explanation: Grouping only visualizes tasks; it does not reassign them.

options.text: It creates separate columns for each person, showing all projects they own at a glance.
options.explanation: Each column represents a team member, so the manager can instantly see who has many or few items.

options.text: It hides projects that are already completed.
options.explanation: Completion visibility depends on the Status filter, not the grouping field.

options.text: It converts the board into a Gantt chart.
options.explanation: Gantt (timeline) view is a separate view type; grouping does not change the visual format.

questions.question: Why is the "Last edited time" property valuable in a documents database?

options.text: It shows which document has the highest word count.
options.explanation: Word count isn’t tracked by this property.

options.text: It indicates the most recent updates, helping the team track progress and avoid working on stale versions.
options.explanation: Seeing the latest edit time lets users prioritize reviewing the freshest content.

options.text: It automatically assigns a reviewer based on the edit timestamp.
options.explanation: Assignment must be set manually or via automation; the timestamp alone doesn’t assign reviewers.

options.text: It creates a backup copy of the document each time it changes.
options.explanation: Notion keeps version history internally, but the property itself doesn’t generate backups.

questions.question: Which property and filter combination would you use to display only meetings that occurred within the past week?

options.text: Created time property with a filter: "Created time is within the past week"
options.explanation: Created time records when the entry was made, and filtering by "is within the past week" shows recent meetings.

options.text: Meeting participants property with a filter: "Participant is me"
options.explanation: Filtering by participant shows who attended, not when the meeting happened.

options.text: Type of meeting property with a filter: "Type is weekly sync"
options.explanation: That filter selects meeting type, not date range.

options.text: Deadline property with a filter: "Deadline is today"
options.explanation: Deadline is unrelated to meeting dates; it’s used for project tasks.

questions.question: Linking a goals database to a 1:1 meeting page primarily helps a manager to:

options.text: Automatically generate performance bonuses.
options.explanation: Bonuses require HR processes; linking goals only provides visibility.

options.text: Keep the employee’s current objectives visible during the meeting, enabling real‑time progress review.
options.explanation: A linked, filtered view shows only that person’s goals, making discussions focused and data‑driven.

options.text: Sync the employee’s calendar with the manager’s calendar.
options.explanation: Calendar syncing is a separate integration; linking goals does not affect calendars.

options.text: Create a backup of all company goals.
options.explanation: Backup is unrelated to the purpose of a linked view in a 1:1 page.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of using an upvote database for team decision‑making?

options.text: It forces every team member to vote on every idea.
options.explanation: Forcing votes can create fatigue; the database simply collects optional input.

options.text: It surfaces the most popular suggestions, allowing managers to prioritize them for discussion or implementation.
options.explanation: Upvotes act as a democratic signal; the items with the most votes get addressed first.

options.text: It automatically assigns tasks to the person who upvoted them.
options.explanation: Assignment must be done manually or via automation; upvotes alone don’t assign work.

options.text: It replaces the need for any formal meeting agenda.
options.explanation: Upvotes complement agendas but don’t eliminate structured meeting planning.

questions.question: Why are relational databases (using relation and rollup properties) more powerful than duplicating information across separate databases?

options.text: They eliminate the need for any manual data entry, making all fields auto‑populate.
options.explanation: Relations and rollups pull linked data, but you still need to create the initial link.

options.text: They ensure a single source of truth; updates made in one place automatically reflect everywhere the data is referenced.
options.explanation: When a property is changed in the original record, all linked rollups update, preventing inconsistencies that duplicated copies cause.

options.text: They allow you to export data to CSV more quickly than separate tables.
options.explanation: Export speed is unrelated to relational linking.

options.text: They automatically generate visual Gantt charts for any linked project.
options.explanation: Gantt charts require a timeline view; relations alone don’t create visualizations.

questions.question: When creating permission groups for cross‑department sharing, what is a common risk if groups are not defined precisely, and how can it be mitigated?

options.text: Users may lose access to their own personal pages; mitigate by disabling groups entirely.
options.explanation: Disabling groups removes the benefit of sharing; the risk is over‑sharing, not loss of personal access.

options.text: Sensitive information could be unintentionally exposed to the wrong team; mitigate by reviewing group membership and using view‑level filters to restrict data.
options.explanation: Broad groups can grant access to confidential pages; careful membership audits and filtered views keep data secure.

options.text: Notion will automatically delete duplicate pages; mitigate by turning off auto‑deletion.
options.explanation: Notion does not delete pages automatically; duplication isn’t the core issue.

options.text: The workspace will exceed its storage quota; mitigate by archiving old pages.
options.explanation: Storage limits are unrelated to permission group definitions.
